QF.id: VI Depository and its Business Partners_47

question.text: For securities held in demat form, the issuer must inform the depository about corporate action dates by when?

answer.text: On the next day on which the information is being sent to the stock exchanges
At least 30 days before the record date
At least 15 days before the book closure
Within 7 days of the board meeting deciding the corporate action

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Issuer shall inform the depository on the next day on which the information is being sent to the stock exchanges where the securities are listed. **The Trap:** 'At least 30 days before the record date' conflates the exchange notification requirement with the depository notification timing. The depository gets informed simultaneously with exchange notification. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VII covers corporate action timelines. Series V-A covers MF dividend distribution dates. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This ensures the depository has the same information as exchanges at the same time — no lag. The depository needs this to prepare the list of beneficial owners as of the record date.
